def: s.m. = śuka and suggā, qq.v.
adj. Going well or gracefully; graceful, elegant; — well rid of; — easy of access, easily passed; — easily understood, plain, intelligible; — s.m. Feces, ordure.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سگ (sag, “dog”). Cognate with Kashmiri ہوٗن (hūn), Kalasha شوںہ (šọ̃́a) and Shina شُن (śun)۔

def: adj. (f. -ā), Easy to be traversed or travelled over, easy of access or approach, accessible, attainable; easy, practicable; plain, intelligible; — going well.
s.f. = S سگمتو सुगमत्व su-gamatva, s.m. Accessibleness, attainableness; easiness, ease, practicableness; plainness, intelligibleness; — easy flow, euphony.
s.m. = S سگمتا सुगमता su-gamatā, s.f. Accessibleness, attainableness; easiness, ease, practicableness; plainness, intelligibleness; — easy flow, euphony.
adj. Easily reached, accessible, easily attainable.
adj. = su-gam, q.v.; — s.m. = sugamatā, q.v.
v.t. To appropriate, to fix on, to apply to.
adj. = su-gandh, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Sweet-smelling, odoriferous, fragrant, spicy, aromatic; — good or pleasant smell, sweet odour, scent, perfume, fragrance, aroma, spiciness
subs: s.m. Any vessel containing sweet odours; a scent-bottle

def: adj. & s.f. = su-gandh, q.v.
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Impregnated or mixed with perfume.

def: adj. & s.f. = su-gandh, q.v.
part. adj. Perfumed, rendered fragrant; sweet-smelling, fragrant ( = su-gandh).
s.f. Fragrance, perfume, agreeable scent (produced by the trituration of perfumes).
s.m. The root of a creeping plant resembling Sarsaparilla in its habit, Periploca indica.
adj. (dialec. & colloq.), corr. of su-ghaṛ, q.v.
s.m. The small; inferiors.
adj. Officious.
s.m. A Buddh; a Bauddh deified preceptor.
s.f. Good gait; good or happy state or condition.
s.m. (f. -ī), A parrot.
s.f. A parrot.
adj. = next, q.v.
adj. Well-formed, well-made, symmetrical, neat, pretty, beautiful, elegant, graceful; accomplished, skilful, clever, expert; eloquent; good, virtuous; meritorious
subs: s.f. Flattery, sycophancy, wheedling, currying favour.

def: adj. = sughaṛ, q.v.
s.f. Lucky hour, auspicious moment, opportune moment, suitable time.
part. adj. Well joined or united; well contrived or devised, well arranged, well managed.

ety: sughaṛ+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+ इका
def: s.f. Neatness, prettiness, beauty, elegance, accomplishment, skilfulness; virtuousness.

ety: sughaṛ+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+ इका
def: s.f. Neatness, prettiness, beauty, elegance, accomplishment, skilfulness; virtuousness.
s.f. Good singing; — a kind of metre.

ety: fem. of aṣg̠ar, v.n. fr. صغر 'to be small'
def: adj. Smaller, less; smallest, least; — s.m. The minor term (of a syllogism).
s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode, sung in the forenoon.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Having a beautiful neck, handsome-necked; — a swan; a goose; a hero; — a sort of weapon; — name of a monkey-king (who, with his army of monkeys headed by Hanumān, assisted Rāmćandra in conquering Rāvan and recovering Sītā); name of one of the four horses of Krishṇ; name of Śiva; and of Indra; name of a particular mountain; name of a particular Nāga or serpent of Pātāla.
adj. Of good omen, lucky
part. adj. Well hidden or concealed, well kept secret, very secret; — adv. Very secretly, privily.
s.f. Secrecy, closeness, good protection.
(rustic) A parrot ( = suggā).
s.m. Sound knowledge, wisdom, intelligence, sagacity; — adj. Well-informed, wise, intelligent.
s.m. A small dim star in the tail of Ursa Major.
s.m. = سہا suhā, q.v
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Red, crimson; saffron-coloured (also written sūʼā); — cloth dyed saffron-colour; — name of a rāg or musical mode
subs: adj. Red as the dye of saffron.

def: s.m. Consumption, ulceration of the lungs.

s.m. Auspiciousness, good fortune; blessedness; happiness, joy, pleasure; the affection of a husband, affection; an act of endearment, a caress; the happy and auspicious state of wife-hood (as opposed to widowhood), the conjugal state, coverture; a kind of ornament or decoration worn by a woman during the lifetime of her husband; a kind of marriage song; congratulations, good wishes
subs: s.m. A medicine used in the milk-fever (parsotī) of lying-in women
s.m. A basket, or a paper folded in the shape of a sugar-loaf, containing cosmetics, paints, perfumes, a comb, bangles and other ornaments, and fruits, &c., presented by a bridegroom to his bride
(-kā), To be deprived of coverture (a woman), to become a widow
s.m. 'The perfume of good luck,' a perfume rubbed on a bride and bridegroom
s.f. A favourable wind; a cool refreshing breeze.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑁄𑀳𑀕𑁆𑀕 (sohagga), from Sanskrit सौभाग्य (saubhāgya). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi سُہاگ (shag) / سُوہاگ (svhag).

def: s.m. Borax ( = sohāgā).
s.f. A married woman whose husband is alive; a woman beloved of her husband; a favourite wife (opp. to duhāgan).
s.m. (f. -inī), A fortunate man; — s.f. = suhāginī, q.v.
s.f. A fortunate woman; a married woman residing in her father's house.
s.f. A fortunate woman; a married woman residing in her father's house.
adj. fem. of suhāyā, q.v.

ety: v.n. dim. form fr. سہل; see sahl
def: s.m. The star Canopus.
s.m. (dialec.) A kind of descriptive or eulogistic song.
s.m. A song of congratulation or good luck, a birth-song (sung by women on the birth of a son).
s.m. Wheat flour kneaded with water, made into very thin broad cakes, and fried in ghī or oil; a thin greasy cake.
s.f. = suhāl, q.v.
v.n. (old H.) = suhāwnā = suhānā, q.v.
adj. = suhānā, q.v.; — 2˚ s.m. = sohān, q.v.

ety: suhā = suhāw˚ = Prk. सुहाव(इ) or सुहावे(इ) = S.सुख+आपय(ति)
def: v.n. To be pleasing, be agreeable (to, -ko); to give pleasure, to please; to be liked, be approved; — to be pleased, be glad, to rejoice; — to be self-satisfied, to give oneself airs, to show off.

ety: suhā˚ = suhāw˚ = Prk. सोहाव(इ) or सोहावे(इ) = S. शोभय (ति), fr. शोभ; or caus. of rt. शुभ्
def: v.n. To shine, &c. ( = sohnā, q.v.); to be beautiful; — v.t. To make beautiful; to ornament, adorn, grace.
adj. (f. -ī), Pleasing, pleasant, agreeable, charming, becoming, graceful.

ety: sahār˚ = Prk. संहार(इ) or संहारे(इ) = S. संहारयति), rt. सम्+हृ
def: v.t. To drag, trail; — sahārnā, To make away with, &c. ( = saṅhārnā, and saṅghārnā, qq.v.).
v.n. To drag, to trail.
adj. = next, q.v.

ety: prob. imperf. part. of suhānā, q.v.
def: adj. (f. -ī), Pleasing, pleasant, agreeable, charming, becoming, graceful.
adj. = suhānā, q.v.).
adj. = Pleasing, &c. ( = suhānā, q.v.); — s.m. Beautifying, embellishment, decoration, ornament, garniture; splendidness, handsomeness.
adj. Pleasing, agreeable, &c. ( = suhānā, q.v.)
s.f. (dialec.) Pleasantness, agreeableness.
adj. & v.n. = suhānā, q.v.
s.f. An agreeable or charming woman.
contrac. of suhāyā, q.v.
adj. (f. suhāʼī), Beautiful, graceful, pleasing, agreeable, &c. ( = suhānā, q.v.)
adj. (dialec.) Easy; feasible.
s.m. = sohlā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Good hearted; very friendly, affectionate, kind; — a kind-hearted person; a friend
subs: s.m. The separation of friends, dissension among friends.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Good hearted; very friendly, affectionate, kind; — a kind-hearted person; a friend
subs: s.m. The separation of friends, dissension among friends.

ety: for A. سہولة, inf. n. of سہل 'To be smooth, or soft, or easy'; see sahl
def: s.f. Smoothness, plainness; ease; softness, gentleness, mildness.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سهولت (suhūlat), from Arabic سُهُولَة (suhūla).

def: adj. Easy-going, slow, lazy.
s.f. pop. form of suhūlat, q.v.
s.f. A needle; needle (of a compass); hand (of a watch); tongue (of a balance); sharp point (of a blade of grass, &c.); a shoot (of corn, &c.); a prickle, thorn
subs: To perform wonders, achieve impossibilities, to make a camel pass through the eye of a needle
s.m. lit. 'Needle-melter'; a very acid kind of lemon (the juice of which is said to dissolve needles
s.m. A species of jasmine, Jasminum auriculatum.

ety: fem. of suʼā, and = Prk. सुइया; S. शुक+इका
def: s.f. A parrot.
adj. Pointed (as a needle), having a sharp point.
s.f. Swelling, tumefaction, intumescence ( = sūjan; cf. also śoth or soth).

ety: S. सूचकः; cf. sūjī, and sūʼī
def: s.m. An implement for perforating or sewing, a large needle, a packing-needle; an awl; a borer, gimlet, auger.
s.m. corr. of sozāk, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Well-informed, wise, intelligent.
adj. (f. -ā), Good-natured, benevolent, kind; good, virtuous; respectable: su-jan-samāj, s.m. The assembly of the good (used chiefly of the Hindū ascetics in their corporate capacity).
s.f. Swelling, intumescence; inflammation.
wety: From سُوجْنا (sūjnā, “to swell”), from Sanskrit शूयते (śūyatē).

def: adj. (in poetry) = su-jān, q.v.
v.t. To cause to swell.
s.f. Benevolence, kindness, affability, urbanity; goodness, virtuousness; respectability.
s.m. & adj. = su-yaś, q.v.
adj. = su-yaśī, q.v.
adj. Of good kind or species; of good caste or race, of good family, well-born.
adj. = سجاتي su-jāti, q.v.
adj. Of good kind or species; of good caste or race, of good family, well-born.
s.f. Seeing, sight, vision, perception.
adj. (f. -ī), Perceived, apparent, visible; — s.m. = sūjh, q.v.
s.f. Showing, pointing out, explaining (syn. dikhāʼī).
v.t. To cause to perceive or understand, to show, point out.
v.t. (dialec.) = sūjhnā, q.v.

ety: sūjh˚ = Prk. सुज्झ(इ) = S. शुध्य(ति), rt. शुध्
def: v.n. To be or become visible (to, -ko), to be seen, to be perceptible, to appear; to seem, to look: to occur (to), to happen
s.m. Preparation, setting right, arrangement, management
subs: (-kā), To prepare, arrange, put to rights, manage.

def: s.f. A needle ( = sūʼī); a small awl (cf. sūjā).
s.m. One who sews, a tailor (syn. darzī).
s.f. Wheaten flour in fine granules, flour ground from the heart of wheat, coarse-ground flour; semolina.
v.t. (dialec.) = sūjhnā, q.v.

ety: sūj˚ = Prk. सुज्ज(इ) = S. शूय(ते), pass. of rt. श्वि
def: v.n. To swell, to rise; to be or become inflamed; to be puffed or puffed out; to pout, to look sulky (usually with the word muṅh expressed).
v.n. (colloq.) = sūjhnā, q.v.
s.m. Wisdom, intelligence, sagacity; — adj. (f. -ā), Well-informed, intelligent, knowing, wise.
s.f. corr. of soznī, q.v.
s.m. = su-yog, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of سجد 'to prostrate oneself' (in prayer, &c.
def: s.m. An act of prostration with the forehead touching the ground, adoration ( = sijda).
s.m. A parrot ( = suggā, q.v.).
adj. Light, bright, white; — s.m. The light half of a month (from new to full moon).
s.m. The planet Venus; — Friday ( = sūk)
s.m. = śūk, and sūʼā, qq.v.
s.m. (Dakkhinī), A market (syn. bāzār; ćauk).
s.m. The planet Venus; Friday ( = suk, q.v.).
s.m. = śūk-kīṭ.
s.m. corr. of sukh, q.v.
s.m. contrac. 1˚ of sukh; — 2˚ of suk-dev; qq.v.
adj. (dialec. or colloq.) = sūkhā, q.v.
s.m. (f. -ī), A fourth part, a quarter; a four-anna piece, a silver coin equal to a quarter of a rupee.
s.m. (f. -ī), A parrot ( = sugā, suggā).
s.m. A parrot; — cloth, clothes; the end or hem of a garment, or of cloth; a helmet, turban; — a sort of perfume; — name of several plants
subs: s.m. Name of the son of Vyāsa (he was the narrator of the Bhāgavat-Purāṇ to king Parīkshit).

def: s.m. Awn (of barley, &c.); a bristle, spicule; sharp hair (of insects); — compassion, clemency, tenderness
s.m. Good or auspicious time; goodness of seasons; good season, time of plenty, plenty, abundance, cheapness.
s.m. name of a class of Pitr̤is or demigods (the manes of the Śudras).
adj. Handsome-necked, &c. ( = su-grīv, q.v.).
s.f. A beautiful girl; a good daughter; — name of a princess (daughter of Śaryāti, and wife of the Rishi Ćyavana).
s.m. A kind of gem; a kind of amber?
adj. & s.m. Doing good, beneficent, benevolent; — easy, practicable, feasible; — doing good, well-doing, beneficence, charity, benevolence.
s.m. A hog, pig ( = śūkar and sūʼar)
subs: s.m. Name of a place of Hindū pilgrimage on the Gomtī (about thirty-two miles south of Ayodhyā-nāth; celebrated as the birthplace of Vishṇu under the form of a boar)
s.m. Name of a division of the infernal regions which is filled with boiling oil.

def: s.m. (f. -ī), A hog; a wild boar (see sūʼar); the hog-deer
subs: s.m. Name of a division of the infernal regions (filled with boiling oil).

def: = śukra; and sūk or suk, qq.v.
s.f. A tractable cow; — the month of May.

adj. Very hard; very unfeeling or cruel.
s.m. = su-kriyā, q.v.

ety: S. su-karmās, m. pl. of su-karma
def: s.m. Name of a class of deities.
adj. Well-doing, &c. ( = su-kar, q.v.).
s.m. = sāvakāś, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of سکت 'to be or become silent'
def: s.f. Silence; — adj. Silent; silenced.
s.m. A good or excellent poet.
s.m. = شکديو śuk-dev, q.v.

s.m. Happiness, pleasure, delight, joy, gratification; comfort, solace, ease, tranquillity; prosperity, easy circumstances, contentment; rest, relief, alleviation; easiness; — heaven, paradise; — water; — adj. Happy, joyful, delighted; delightful, agreeable, sweet; easy; virtuous, pious
subs: (˚kha+ar˚), adv. For the sake of happiness, or ease, &c.
adj. & s.m. Seeking or wishing for happiness; desirous of ease; — a seeker after happiness; one who is desirous of ease
s.m. An easy chair; a couch; a palanquin
(˚kha+ās˚), adj. & s.m. Pleasant-flavoured, agreeable, delightful; — pleasant flavour, pleasantness, enjoyment
s.m. Blissful place, abode of happiness, paradise
s.m. Abode of comfort, ease, or happiness ( = sukh-dhām)
s.m. One who devotes himself to enjoyment, a pleasure-seeker
adj. & s.m. Fortunate, happy, delighted, in the enjoyment of ease; — a fortunate person, &c.
adj. & s.m. Enjoying pleasure or happiness, luxurious; — a luxurious person, &c.
s.m. A kind of palanquin; an easy chair; a couch
s.f. The attainment or enjoyment of happiness, or ease, &c.
vulg. sukh-pūrbak, adv. With ease or pleasure, without difficulty, easily
s.m. A piece of leather placed on the sole within a shoe to protect the foot from friction
s.m. Ease, rest, peace, tranquillity; leisure

vulg. sukhad, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Ease-affording, pleasure-giving, comfortable; salubrious; pleasant; comforting, solacing; — one who (or that which) confers happiness, or affords ease or comfort, &c.; ease-giver, comforter; — an epithet of Vishṇu
s.f. A woman who affords happiness; — a courtezan of Indra's heaven
s.m. Name of a very superior and delicious kind of rice
adj. = sukh-da, q.v.; — s.m. The act of imparting happiness, or comfort, or ease, &c.; beneficence
sukh-dāyak, sukh-dāʼī, and sukh-dāyī, adj. & s.m. = sukha-da, q.v.
vulg. sukh-darsan, s.m. The shrub Crinum asiaticum or C. zeylanicum (the juice of which is given for the ear-ache)
s.m. Abode of happiness or tranquillity, &c.; blissful place, paradise
adj. Imparting happiness, pleasure-yielding; — s.m. Happiness and unhappiness, joy and sorrow, prosperity and poverty
vulg. sukh-rāsī, s.m. Accumulation of happiness (an epithet of the Deity)
adj. Easy to be accomplished, easy of attainment; easy to be cured; easy to be managed or subdued
s.m. 'Ocean of pleasure'; an epithet of the Bhāgavat Purāṇ; and of any popular deity (as Rāmćandra, &c.)
s.m. 'Place of happiness,' an epithet of paradise or heaven
s.m. 'Accumulation of happiness'; an epithet of the Deity
adv. In comfort, comfortably, at ease; leisurely
vulg. sukh-sīl, adj. Of a happy or pleasant disposition
sukh-kārī, sukh-kar, adj. & s.m. Causing happiness or pleasure, imparting happiness; generous, benevolent; delightsome; — one who imparts happiness or pleasure, a generous or benevolent man
To take rest or repose, to lie down, to sleep
s.m. A minute portion of happiness, a trace of happiness
s.m. A little pleasure, a small portion of happiness, insignificant pleasure
adj. Consisting of happiness, full of joy or pleasure, blissful, joyous
s.m. 'Abode of bliss,' an epithet of the Supreme Being
or sukh-nīṅd, s.f. Good or sound sleep, refreshing slumber
(˚kha+up˚), adj. Contributing to or producing happiness, pleasure, or ease, &c.
(˚kha+ud˚), s.m. The occurrence or the realization of happiness or pleasure
(˚kha+ić˚), s.f. Hope or desire of happiness.

ety: Prk. सुक्खओ or सुक्खअं; S. शुष्क+कः, rt शुष्
def: adj. (f. -ī), Dry, dried, dried up, parched, withered; emaciated, sapless, juiceless; without advantage or profit; — s.m. Dry land, terra firma; a dry year, dry season, drought; dry tobacoo (eaten with betel-leaf); a consumption, atrophy; the croup
subs: s.m. A dry answer; a flat refusal
s.m. A rice-field burnt up by the sun's rays
lit. 'Rain to fall on dry rice-fields'; rain to fall opportunely; to get new life, to be revived
(-ko), To be in a decline, to waste away, become emaciated
adv. By land (as opp. to water).

def: adj. = sukhad, q.v.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. sukh.
s.m. Abode of happiness or ease, pleasant place, blissful abode ( = sukh-dhām).
adj. (f. -ī), Easy (syn. āsān).
adj. corr. of sūkshma, q.v.
v.t. To cause to dry or dry up; to cause to wither; to cause to pine away or become emaciated; to cause to evaporate; to dry, to dry up; to parch; to evaporate; to wither; to emaciate; to kill

s.f. See s.v. sukh.

adj. Well-pleased, glad, happy, tranquil, at ease, comfortable, contented; possessing happiness or pleasure; pleasant

def: part. Pleased, delighted, comforted, tranquil, happy ( = sukhī).
adj. Well-pleased, glad, happy, tranquil, at ease, comfortable, contented; possessing happiness or pleasure; pleasant
subs: adj. & s.m. Of a happy or contented disposition; — a happy or contented disposition.

def: v.t. = sukhānā, q.v.
adj. = sukh-may, q.v.s.v. sukh.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), corr. of su-kumār, q.v. (and cf. sukwār).
v.n. = sūkhnā, q.v.

ety: sūkh˚ = Prk. सुक्ख(इ) or सुक्खे(इ), fr. S. शुष्क
def: v.n. To dry, dry up; to evaporate; to be parched; to wither, to pine away, languish, fall away, to become emaciated; to shrivel, to dwindle; — to be kept waiting, be kept out in the cold
s.m. See s.v. sukh.
s.m. See s.v. sukh.

ety: for A. سخرة suḵẖrat, inf. n. of سخر 'to mock at,' &c.
def: s.m. Mockery, scoff, derision, ridicule; — one who is derided or laughed at, a laughingstock, a ridiculous person; — one who is compelled to labour without recompense or wages, one who is pressed to service.

ety: for A. سخريّة, fr. suḵẖra, q.v.
def: s.m. Mockery, scoff, derision, ridicule ( = suḵẖra).

ety: old P. sahvan; Pehl. śakhun; Zend sāqeni, rt. śaq or śaṅh = शस्
def: s.m. Speech, language, discourse, word, words; — thing, business, affair (syn. bāt)
subs: adj. Adorning language or discourse, eloquent
s.f. Eloquence, elocution, display of language
adj. & s.m. Talking; fluent, eloquent; — a fluent orator
adj. & s.m. Adhering to one's word; bigoted, prejudiced; — a poet, an author; — one who adheres to his word, or to his opinions
s.f. Obstinate adherence to one's word or opinion
s.m. 'A prop-word,' an expletive (syn. takya-kalām, q.v.)
s.m. Critic, hypercritic; informer, tale-bearer, spy; calumniator
adj. & s.m. Skilled in language, eloquent; — a scholar; an orator; a poet
s.f. Knowledge of language, philology; eloquence, oratory
To pledge (one's) word, to promise, to vow
(-par), To ask, question, interrogate; to beg
adj. & s.m. Lengthening a discourse, prolix; — an orator, a rhetorician:-suḵẖan-rānī, s.f. Oratory, eloquence, elocution, rhetoric
adj. Intelligent, penetrating; attending to what is said; eloquent
adj. & s.m. Speaking (readily); intelligent; — a poet; a relater of tales; a liar
s.m. Orator, speaker; a poet; one who makes words to suit his purpose, a deceiver
s.f. Oratory, elocution, eloquence, address; — fabrication, invention; defamation
adj. & s.m. Weighing one's words, prudent; — one who weighs his words, a wise or prudent man; a poet
adj. & s.m. Knowing or conversant in language, eloquent; a scholar; an orator; a poet
adj. Quick of apprehension, intelligent; conversant in language, &c. ( = suḵẖan-shinās, q.v.)
adj. & s.m. Speaking; praising; eloquent; — an orator; a poet
s.f. Speaking, speech; oratory, rhetoric, eloquence; praise; poetry; the poetic art
adj. Not attending to advice, inattentive
s.f. Inattention to advice, inattention, heedlessness

def: v.t. To cause to be dried, to have or get dried, &c. (see sukhānā).
part. Well spoken of, of good report, very renowned, celebrated, famous.
s.f. Good report, fame, celebrity.
s.f. = sūkā, q.v.
adj. Of the market.
s.f. Good report, good character; celebration; — an epithet of a certain hymn.
adj. (dialec.), Dry, &c. ( = sūkhā, q.v.).
wety: Either from Classical Persian سکا (sakā) or a modern learned borrowing from an ancient Indo-Iranian language referring to the same nomadic peoples.

ety: v.n. fr. سکن 'to be or become still,' &c.
def: s.m. A rudder, helm (of a ship or boat)
s.m. pl. (of sākin, q.v.), Inhabitants, dwellers.

ety: rel. n. fr. A. sukkān, q.v.
def: s.m. Helmsman, steersman; quartermaster (of a ship).
s.m. Sugar (cf. sakkar).
s.m. contrac. of śūka-kīṭ, q.v.s.v. śūka.
adj. & s.m. = suk, and śukla, q.v.

r: śukla, śukl, śukal, śukul, sukal, sukul
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Light, bright; white, clean, pure, unsullied; — white colour, whiteness; name of a sect of Brāhmaṇs (who derive their subsistence by serving, or by begging from Brāhmans, and are regarded as unsullied); an individual of that sect; — the twenty-fourth of the astronomical period called yoga; the light half of a month (from new to full moon); — silver; — fresh butter; — a disease of the cornea, albugo (cf. śukra)
subs: s.m. The light half of the month (the fortnight of the moon's increase; opp. to kr̤ishṇ-paksh)
or śukl-pakshīya, adj. Relating to the light half of the month
s.f. The fourth day of the light half of a month
adj. Making white, whitening; — s.m. Soap
s.m. lit. 'Having a white throat'; a kind of gallenule or water-hen.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَکْل (šakl), borrowed from Arabic شَكْل (šakl).

def: s.f. Brightness, whiteness, fairness ( = śuklatā, q.v.).
s.f. = S شکلتو शुक्लत्व śuklatva, s.m. Brightness; whiteness, &c.
s.m. = S شکلتا शुक्लता śuklatā, s.f. Brightness; whiteness, &c.
adj. & s.m. Making white, whitening; — soap.

ety: inf. n. of سقم 'to be or become diseased,' &c.
def: s.m. Disease, disorder, sickness; defect, flaw.
v.n. (dialec.) = sūkhnā, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Apabhramsa सक्कइ (sakkaï), from Prakrit 𑀲𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀇 (sakkaï), from Sanskrit श॒क्नोति॑ (śaknóti), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *ćaknáwti, from Proto-Indo-European *ḱek- + *-néwti.

def: v.n. (dialec. or colloq.) = sūkhnā, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of سکر 'to be or become intoxicated'
def: s.m. Intoxication, inebriation; — any intoxicating drink, wine.
s.m. = suk, and śukr, q.v.
s.m. An earthen cup or bowl.
adj. = sukṛā, q.v.

adj. & s.m. Bright, resplendent; white, pure; — The planet Venus, or its regent (regarded as the son of Bhrigu and preceptor of the Daityas); Friday; — the month Jyeshṭha or Jeṭh (May-June); name of Agni; — semen virile, seed (of animals), sperm; male and female energy; a morbid affection of the iris (change of colour, &c., accompanied by imperfect vision)
subs: (˚ra+āć˚), s.m. The regent of the planet Venus and preceptor of the Daityas
s.m. The origin, or the mixing, of sperm

def: s.m. Contracting, contraction; shrinking, shrivelling, wrinkling.
s.f. Pitifulness, tenderness, compassion
subs: s.m. lit. 'The receptacle or abode of compassion,' an epithet of the Deity.

def: part. adj. (f. -ā), Well done, well performed; easily accomplished; treated with kindness, befriended, assisted; — virtuous, pious; fortunate, auspicious; — s.m. (for S. su-kr̤itam), Good or virtuous act, kindness, friendliness; bounty; — virtue, moral merit; fortune, auspiciousness
subs: To perform a good or virtuous action, to do good, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. Acting well or kindly, benevolent, benign; virtuous, pious, good, meritorious; fortunate, auspicious.
s.f. Good action, good work, moral or religious observance, meritorious deed, merit.
s.m. Friday.
s.f. A kind of jasmine, Jasminum auriculatum; a sort of perfume; small cardamoms.

adj. (f. -ā), Subtle, minute, atomic, atom-like; little, small; slender, thin, fine, attenuated; delicate, tender; exquisite, refined; nice; sharp, acute; shrill; ingenious; artful, crafty; accurate, correct, exact, precise; — s.m. An atom; fine thread; subtlety; craft, fraud; an epithet of the Supreme Spirit; — the clearing-nut plant
subs: s.f. Sharp wit, acute intellect, mental acumen; — adj. Sharp-witted, witty, acute, shrewd, intelligent, penetrating, ingenious; abstracted
and sūkshma-darśī, adj. & s.m. Sharp-sighted, keen-eyed; of acute discernment; acute, quick, intelligent; — a sharp-sighted person, an acute person
s.m. (in Philos.) 'The atom-like body,' the subtle body which is invested by, and is the archetype or pattern of, the gross material frame.

def: adj. incorrect for sūkshma, q.v.
s.f. = S سوکشمتو सूक्ष्मत्व sūkshmatva, s.m. Thinness, fineness; minuteness, subtlety; acuteness; shrillness.
s.m. = S سوکشمتا सूक्षमता sūkshmatā, s.f. Thinness, fineness; minuteness, subtlety; acuteness; shrillness.
s.m. corr. of sūkt, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Dry, dried up, lean, thin, emaciated.
adj. = sukṭā, q.v.
s.m. What is well spoken; a sentence; an invocation, a Vedic metrical prayer; a hymn

ety: the preceding, with इका for कः
def: s.f. Dried fish; — a withered old woman.
s.f. Oyster; mussel, cockle; snail.

s.f. A pearl-oyster; an oyster-shell; a small shell; a muscle, a cockle; a conch-shell; a portion of the skull (used as a cup, &c.); a curl or feather on a horse's neck or breast; a sort of perfume; a disease of the cornea; a weight of two karshas or four tolās
subs: s.m. Name of one of the seven principal mountains or mountain ranges of India.

def: adj. & s.m. Of good family, well-born; — a good or virtuous family; — name of a caste of Brāhmaṇs.
adj. (f. -ā or -ī) & s.m. Beautifully young or youthful; very soft or tender; smooth; delicate; elegant; — a beautiful youth.
s.f. Softness, tenderness, smoothness, delicateness; youthfulness; elegance.

ety: inf. n. of سکن 'to be still,' &c.
def: s.m. Quiet, quietude, tranquillity, rest, peace; cessation; a mute.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سُکُون (sukūn), from Arabic سُكُون (sukūn).

ety: for A. سکونة, fr. sukūn, q.v.
def: s.f. Dwelling, residence, habitation; — rest, tranquillity, contentment ( = sukūn).
s.m. corr. of 1˚ śūkar; 2˚ sukar, qq.v.

ety: inf. n. of سکت 'to be silent'
def: s.f. Silence, quietness, peace.
s.m. A kind of vetch.
adj. Soft, tender; delicate, feeble.
s.m. Friday (see s.v. suk).

s.m. (f. p), A sharp or acute pain; colic; stomach-ache; indigestion; gout; rheumatism; — any sharp or pointed weapon; a pike, dart, spear, lance, spike; a thorn, prickle; the point of a spear, &c.; — sympathy, compassion, tenderness.
s.m. Condition, position, situation.

s.m. A sharp or acute pain; pain in the stomach, colic; rheumatism; gout; — any sharp or pointed weapon, a pike, dart, spear, lance; a trident; a stake; a spit; an iron pin; — a banner, ensign; death, dying; the ninth astronomical yoga
subs: adj. & s.m. Holding a trident in the hand; — an epithet of Śiva
s.m. Name of a division of the infernal regions
s.m. lit. 'Enemy of colic,' the castor-oil plant, Ricinus communis
s.m. 'Destroyer of pain,' an epithet of Śiva
s.m. or śūl-hat, s.f. 'Removing colic'; assafœtida.

def: s.f. A spit; a stake (for impaling criminals; — see sūlī); — a harlot; — a brick-kiln.
adj. = su-labh, q.v.
adj. Easy to be obtained, easy of acquisition; easy to be perceived or effected; easy of access; attainable, feasible, easy
subs: (˚bha+av˚), adj. Easily gaining room or admission.

def: s.f. Easiness of acquisition or attainment, feasibleness.
adj. = su-labh, q.v.
s.m. = su-lakshaṇ, q.v.

ety: sulag˚ prob. = S. संलग(ति), rt. लग् with सम्
def: v.n. To be kindled, be ignited, be set light to; to light; to burn without smoke or flame, to burn clearly or brightly; to be inflamed, be excited, be roused, be irritated.
s.m. pl. (of ṣāliḥ, q.v.), The just, the virtuous, the pious; men of wisdom or counsel.
prop. n. Solomon (the son of David).
adj. Of or belonging to Solomon; — s.f. The state or dignity of Solomon; — s.m. ( = sulaimānī patthar), The onyx.
v.n. To be or become unravelled, to be disentangled; to be solved; to be analysed, tested or tried; to be settled, be set right again
subs: part. (of sulajhnā and suljhānā), Ready, disentangled, &c.; tested, proved, experienced.

def: adj. = su-lakshaṇ, q.v.

s.m. A good or distinct mark; an auspicious mark or sign or omen; good conduct; excellence; beauty; a determining; — adj. Clearly distinguished by marks; of good omen, auspicious, favourable, promising, fortunate; well-behaved; clever, expert; beautiful; excellent.

s.m. A good or distinct mark; an auspicious mark or sign or omen; good conduct; excellence; beauty; a determining; — adj. Clearly distinguished by marks; of good omen, auspicious, favourable, promising, fortunate; well-behaved; clever, expert; beautiful; excellent.
adj. = su-lakshaṇ.
v.t. To cause to sleep, to put to sleep; to hush, lull, quiet, calm, allay; to lay, repress, quell, subdue; to kill, murder
adv. Three and three together, three at a time, by threes.
adj. 'Of, or relating to, three things'; comprising or composed of three (radical) letters (a word), triliteral; made up, or consisting of, three parts, triple; three-sided, triangular.

v.t. To turn round again, to restore to a right position, to set right again; to arrange or settle (a matter or affair, with another); — v.n. To come or turn round again to a right position, to get or come right again
s.m. 'The backbone'; the loins; the back (esp. the lumbar portion); (met.) offspring.
adj. Of or relating to the loins, lumbar; proceeding from the loins of, descended or sprung from; legitimate.
s.m. A small ball of tobacco smoked in a ḥuqqa without the intervention of a tile
subs: v.t. To burn to ashes; to consume
v.n. To be consumed; to be utterly destroyed or ruined.

def: v.t. To light, kindle, ignite, to cause to burn brightly; to inflame, incite, stir up, rouse.

ety: fr. muṣālaḥat, 'making peace,' inf. n. iii of صلح; see ṣalāh
def: s.f. Peace, reconciliation, truce, agreement, concord; compromise; treaty
subs: s.f. A voluntary reconciliation; a voluntary compromise
s.f. The act of breaking the peace; a breach of the peace
adj. & s.m. Peaceful; peace-making, amicable, conciliatory; — a peaceful or conciliatory person; peace-maker
s.f. The state of being amicable or peaceable, amicableness, conciliatoriness, &c.
s.f. Perfect reconciliation; permanent or durable peace
(-se), To make peace (with), to be reconciled (to); to make a truce, come to terms (with), &c.
or kar-denā (-meṅ), or sulḥ karwānā (-meṅ), To make peace (between), to reconcile
s.f. Perfect reconciliation; definitive treaty; — adj. & s.m. Desirous of conciliating all, peaceful, friends with all; — one who is desirous of conciliating all, a peaceful man
s.m. A written deed of pacification; a treaty of peace; a deed of compromise
v.n. Peace to be concluded; reconciliation to take place.

def: s.m. Friendship.
adv. Peaceably, pacifically, amicably.
s.f. An impaling stake; — gallows, gibbet; — (fig.) acute suffering, torture, agony; risk of life; hard labour
subs: To suffer torture or agony (like that of impalement); to be in constant fear of (one's) life
or sūlī ćaṛhānā, or sūlī denā (-ko), To impale; to hang; to crucify; to torture, give pain (to)
s.m. One who impales; — a hangman, executioner.

def: adj. Suffering sharp pain; having the colic; — s.m. An epithet of Śiva (as holding a trident).

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Suffering sharp pain; afflicted with colic; suffering from rheumatism; — armed with a spear or pike, carrying a lance or stake &c.; — one who suffers sharp pain, &c.; — a spearman, pikeman, lancer; an epithet of Mahādeʼo or Śiva.
s.f. See سولي sūlī.
corr. of śūlī and sulī, qq.v.
v.t. To unravel, to disentangle; to solve (a difficulty or a problem); to analyse, to examine, test, prove; to set right or straight again, to settle, arrange.

r: suljhāʼo, suljhāwā, suljhā, nā, āʼo, āwā
def: s.m. Unravelling, disentanglement; solution; settlement.

r: suljhāwā, suljhāʼo, suljhā, nā, āʼo, āwā
def: s.m. Unravelling, disentanglement; solution; settlement.
s.m. A ladder; staircase.
s.m. A third, a third part or portion; — a kind of Arabic hand-writing.
s.f. = sursurī, q.v.

ety: v.n. fr. سلط 'to overcome'; 'to possess dominion,' &c.
def: s.m. Sultan, emperor, king, prince, sovereign, ruler.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سلطان (sultān), from Arabic سُلْطَان (sulṭān).

ety: for A. سلطانة sult̤ānat, fem. of sultān
def: s.f. Empress; queen.
adj. Of or belonging or relating to a sovereign, royal, regal, princely; — s.f. Dignity or office of sult̤ān; sovereignty, empire, dominion ( = salt̤anat); — a kind of broadcloth.
corr. of sult̤ānī, q.v.

s.m. Journey, road, way; institution, rule, mode, manner; behaviour or conduct (to or towards), treatment, usage; civility, attention, kindness; intercourse, amicable intercourse or footing, good terms or understanding (with); cessation of hostilities, peace
subs: adv. With civility or kindness, kindly; properly, well; in amicable intercourse, on good terms, peaceably (e.g. sulūk-se pesh ānā, To treat or receive kindly; to behave properly or well; — sulūk-se rahnā, To live on good terms)
(-se), To proceed (with); to act or behave (towards), to treat; to treat kindly, to befriend, to behave well (to); to be reconciled (to), make peace (with).

def: v.t. To cause to be put to sleep, to have (one) put to sleep, &c. (see sulānā and sonā).

ety: Pehl. śumb, śum; Zend śafa; S. शफ
def: s.m. A hoof, hoofed foot
subs: To pare the hoofs (of, -ke); — to trip, stumble (a horse).

def: s.m. A flower.

ety: prob. corr. of A. شوم shūm
def: s.m. A miser, niggard.

ety: v.n. fr. سمق 'to be tall or high,' 'to overtop'
def: adj. Pure sheer, unmixed
subs: s.m. A vessel for rice (it consists of two boiling pots, one fitting into the other, the inner pot being perforated full of small holes: when the rice is boiled the inner vessel is lifted out, leaving the water strained off in the exterior pot).

def: adj. Well-garlanded, possessed of a good garland.

adj. & s.m. Very charming, fair, beautiful, handsome; of good disposition, good-minded, well-disposed, benevolent; well-pleased, satisfied, happy; — a learned man, a teacher; a student of the Vedas; wheat; the thorn-apple, Datura metel; a flower; the great-flowered jasmine
subs: and suman-dhanush, s.m. Epithet of the bow of Kāmdeva

def: s.m. An epithet of lake Mānas.
s.f. The great-flowered jasmine ( = su-man).
adj. Very fortunate or auspicious, bringing good fortune; abounding in sacrifices.
adj. Very captivating, very charming, very agreeable or pleasing, delightful, beautiful.
adj. & s.m. Well deliberated, well-advised; — good advice ( = next, q.v.).
s.m. = S سمنترنا सुमन्त्रणा su-mantraṇā, s.f. Goodadvice, wise counsel.
s.f. = S سمنترن सुमन्त्रण su-mantraṇ, s.m. Goodadvice, wise counsel.
adj. Dying easily.
1˚ adj. corr. of su-mar, q.v.; — 2˚ s.m. corr. of shumār, q.v.

ety: Prk. सुमरणअं; S. स्मरणीयं, rt. स्मृ
def: v.t. To remember, recollect, call to mind, keep in memory; to invoke, to call on (God); to mention.
adj. Of good or correct conduct, good, upright, pious.
adj. Well-disposed, kindly disposed, friendly
subs: An epithet of any person who is remarkable for friendliness.

def: s.f. Name of the wife of Sagara (mother of 60,000 sons); — name of the wife of Vishṇu-yaśas and mother of Kalkī.

s.f. Good mind, good understanding, sound sense, judgment; good disposition, goodwill, friendliness, friendship, kindness, benevolence; good conscience; favour of the gods, favour, blessing, gift; desire, wish; — adj. Of a good understanding, wise, clever, intelligent; of a good conscience; of a good or kind disposition, benevolent.
s.m. A ponderous implement of iron for cleaving blocks of stone; rammer (of a cannon), ramrod, sponge-staff ( = sumbhā); a peg, spike, punch
s.f. A hoof ( = sum, q.v.).
s.m. A hole made in a wall.
s.m. Name of a demon (brother of Niśumbh) slain by Durgā
subs: s.m. lit. 'The city of the demon Śumbha'; a town and district (the modern Sambhalpūr in the district of Goṇdwānā).

def: s.m. = sumbā, q.v.

ety: v.n. fr. سنبل 'to put forth ears' (corn
def: s.m. A plant of sweet odour, spikenard (to which the Persians compare the locks of a mistress); — the hyacinth; maiden-hair
subs: s.m. A species of spikenard, Nardostachys jatamansi
s.m. The spikenard, Valeriana jatamansi; the hyacinth.
